question_text: A country is choosing between building a new hydroelectric power station or a new coal power station. Which statement best explains why hydroelectric power is better for long-term energy security?

options.label: A
options.text: Hydroelectric power is cheaper to build than coal power stations.

options.label: B
options.text: Water is a renewable resource that will not run out, whereas coal reserves are finite and will eventually be depleted.

options.label: C
options.text: Hydroelectric power stations produce no pollution and coal stations pollute the air.

options.label: D
options.text: Hydroelectric power can generate electricity 24 hours a day, while coal power stations can only work during the day.

explanation: Energy security means having reliable, long-term access to energy. Although option C is true, it addresses environmental impact, not security. Option D is incorrect (coal can run day and night). Option A is debatable and not the main security issue. Option B is correct because hydroelectric uses water—a renewable resource—so supply is guaranteed for the foreseeable future, whereas coal is non-renewable and will eventually run out, threatening future energy security.
